Add items system (TMs, tokens), sharding, token-based evolution, PokemonIcon component

- Add TM sprites (18 types) and bwicons sprite sheet with symlinks
- Add shared move-data.ts mapping moves to types for TM display
- Add item types (TM, token) and OwnedItem to shared types
- Add owned_items DB table and server endpoints (add/remove items, evolve in-place)
- Add Items screen showing TMs and tokens grouped with counts
- Add Items button to main menu
- Pack opening now grants 1 random TM alongside 3 pokemon
- Add PokemonIcon component using bwicons sprite sheet (32x32 grid, 16 cols)
- Add sharding: destroy a pokemon to get its token
- Rework evolution: 1 pokemon + 3 tokens -> evolved form (keeps IVs/nature)
- Make main menu buttons area scrollable

commit.message:
fix: accept 'copilot' or 'hey copilot', improved fuzzy matching

Wake phrase matching now:
- Accepts just 'copilot' (or any phrase without leading fillers like 'hey')
  as well as the full configured phrase, so both 'Copilot' and 'Hey Copilot'
  trigger activation.
- Uses 40% edit-distance tolerance per word (max(1, round(len*0.4))):
  'hey'(3)→1 edit, 'copilot'(7)→3 edits, covering mishearings like 'coballot'.
- Guards against empty normalized text causing false positives.
- Only emits 'heard' event when listener is still active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Pipeline] Extract compliance decisions lookup into shared ComplianceQueries helper (#408)

Closes #406

## Summary

Eliminates duplicate latest-decision-per-scan query logic that existed
independently in both `ComplianceEndpoints.cs` and
`Compliance.cshtml.cs`.

## Changes

- **`PRDtoProd/Data/ComplianceQueries.cs`** _(new)_ — static
`GetLatestDecisionsAsync(TicketDbContext)` helper that encapsulates the
decisions query, in-memory grouping, and `OrderByDescending`
tie-breaking
- **`ComplianceEndpoints.GetAllScans`** — replaced 11-line inline block
with a single `await ComplianceQueries.GetLatestDecisionsAsync(db)` call
- **`Compliance.cshtml.cs OnGetAsync`** — replaced 9-line inline block
with the same shared call; also fixes a latent null-safety bug (page
model was using `.First()` which throws if the group is somehow empty;
now uses `.FirstOrDefault()` consistently)

## PRD Fidelity

No PRD traceability section — this is a code quality / bug issue
(`#406`) identified by the Duplicate Code Detector workflow, not a
PRD-decomposed feature. The fix is purely internal; no API contracts or
page behaviour are changed.

## Test Results

```
Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s).
Passed! Failed: 0, Passed: 165, Skipped: 0, Total: 165
```

commit.message:
ci: add sanitizer corpus scan workflow

- Create .github/workflows/sanitizer-corpus-scan.yml (3 jobs):
  1. build-tools: Build iccanalyzer-lite + colorbleed_tools from xsscx/research
     with ASAN+UBSAN+Coverage instrumentation
  2. scan-icc-profiles: Run 95 ICC profiles through iccanalyzer-lite (-a mode)
     and iccToXml_unsafe, collect ASAN/UBSAN findings per-file
  3. scan-icc-xml: Run 116 ICC XML files (42 named + 74 AFL-minimized) through
     iccFromXml_unsafe, plus round-trip validation (XML→ICC→XML)

- Create .github/copilot-instructions.md with repo context
- Update README.md with comprehensive structure documentation

Triggers: push to graphics/icc/ or xml/icc/, workflow_dispatch
Artifacts: per-file ASAN/UBSAN reports, coverage data, markdown summaries

commit.message:
Replace legacy I2S with async DAC continuous DMA driver

Migrate fw_audio_output.c from the deprecated legacy I2S DAC path to
ESP-IDF's DAC continuous driver using asynchronous mode. The previous
synchronous/cyclic approach suffered from DMA descriptor pool corruption
during cyclic-to-acyclic transitions, causing ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT on every
write after the first.

Async mode avoids this entirely:
- DMA runs in a permanent ring of 8×256-byte descriptors
- ISR on_convert_done callback fires per descriptor completion
- A fill task on core 0 reads from a 4096-byte lock-free SPSC ring buffer
- Render loop pushes audio into the ring (non-blocking, overflow drops oldest)
- When ring is empty, fill task outputs midscale silence (0x80)

Results verified on hardware:
- A440 test tone: 40 FPS, 1.9ms display + 0.8ms audio
- forest-wind: 10.9 FPS, 89.9ms display + 1.7ms audio (display-bound)
- Clean boot with zero errors, clean shader transitions
- Zero compiler warnings in fullclean build

Also removes the -Wno-cpp/-Wno-deprecated-declarations workaround from
CMakeLists.txt since the legacy driver/i2s.h include is gone.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive fuzz tests for Wayland wire protocol

Implemented fuzz tests for all critical wire format primitives:
- FuzzDecodeHeader: validates header parsing robustness
- FuzzDecodeInt32/Uint32: tests integer encoding/decoding
- FuzzDecodeFixed: validates fixed-point number handling
- FuzzDecodeString: tests string marshaling with arbitrary input
- FuzzDecodeArray: validates array encoding with padding
- FuzzEncodeMessage: tests complete message encoding

Each fuzz test includes:
- Seed corpus with valid and edge-case inputs
- Roundtrip validation (encode→decode→compare)
- Error handling verification

Validation:
- All fuzz tests pass with 5s fuzzing runs
- Achieved 100% baseline coverage
- No crashes or panics detected with 1M+ executions
- go test ./... passes (19/19 packages)
- go vet clean
- Race detector clean

Addresses audit finding: Wire protocol encoding lacks fuzz testing.
The EncodeMessage function was already refactored to helper functions
(encodeArguments, encodeArgument), reducing complexity to acceptable
levels. This change adds the recommended fuzz tests for protocol
robustness verification.

Files modified: 1 new, 3 updated
Tests: PASS
Diff: +244 lines (fuzz tests), zero regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix branch name not updating when switching chats

RefreshCodingProjectState was async void with no staleness guard.
When switching chats rapidly, the old git query could complete after
the new one and overwrite the correct branch name.

- Change RefreshCodingProjectState from async void to async Task
- Await it in LoadChatAsync and SendMessage (the critical paths)
- Use _ = fire-and-forget with version counter for void callers
- Remove implicit call from OnCurrentChatChanged; callers handle it
  explicitly so async work is properly owned

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix worktree toggle becoming unresponsive after navigation

The AsyncRelayCommand's default CanExecute behavior (returns false
while the task is running) conflicts with the IsEnabled binding on the
toggle buttons. After navigating to an existing chat and back, the
command's CanExecute state could prevent the buttons from re-enabling
even though IsWorktreeLocked correctly returns false.

Adding AllowConcurrentExecutions=true makes the command's CanExecute
always return true, relying instead on the IsEnabled binding and the
method's own guards (CurrentChat != null check) for state control.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add weekly-repo-map workflow (#243)

Adds a new weekly workflow that generates an ASCII tree map
visualization of the repository's file structure and size distribution.

The workflow:
- Runs every Monday via schedule, or on-demand via workflow_dispatch
- Uses standard bash tools (find, du) for language-agnostic analysis
- Generates proportional size bars and file counts in a visual tree
- Creates a GitHub issue with the tree map, key statistics, and file type breakdown
- Closes the previous week's issue automatically

Adapted from repo-tree-map.md in Peli's Agent Factory (gh-aw).

fix: clear actualTime and calledIn fields on void/clear; add both clear options

- All clear operations (void batch, clear this time, clear all times)
  now also reset actualTime and calledIn which were being left behind,
  causing the checkpoint cell to still show a time after clearing
- clearAllCheckpointTimes action exposed alongside clearCheckpointRunner
- HeadsUpGrid routes onClear to the appropriate action based on clearAll flag

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(bubble): Platform Bubble architecture — docker exec on persistent project containers

- platform/projects/container.py: ProjectContainer lifecycle (create/start/stop/exec/build/push)
  - docker exec sf-{id} instead of docker run --rm (ephemeral)
  - 1 persistent container per project with named volume sf-workspace-{id}
  - auto-detect base image from project tech stack (node/python/rust/go/java)
  - build() → push() → deploy pipeline support
  - DOCKER_HOST support for socket proxy
- platform/tools/sandbox.py: migrate run_in_project_docker() to use ProjectContainer.exec()
  - was: docker run --rm (ephemere, state lost between calls)
  - now: docker exec on persistent container (build cache, venvs accumulate)
  - fallback to direct subprocess if docker exec fails
- docker-compose.dev.yml: Platform Bubble compose (socket proxy + isolated networks)
  - tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy: whitelist exec/list/start/stop, forbid delete/swarm
  - sf-internal: platform + postgres + redis (no project containers)
  - sf-projects: platform + project containers (isolated from DB)
- Dockerfile.platform: Debian 13 (python:3.13-slim), docker-cli only, rtk, Node 22
- Dockerfile.project.{node,python,rust}: base images for project containers

Why: docker exec on persistent container vs docker run --rm:
- State accumulates between agent calls (node_modules, cargo cache, venv)
- Landlock works natively in Debian-based containers
- Container becomes deployable artefact (build → push → run)
- No DinD: platform talks to HOST daemon via socket proxy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve wake phrase accuracy with --prompt hint and fuzzy matching

  toward 'Copilot' instead of phonetic guesses like 'coballot' or 'co lot'.

  against transcribed words within ~25% edit distance, so slight mishearings
  still trigger activation even if --prompt isn't enough.

commit.message:
fix: macOS native build uses Mac Catalyst target for UIKit

The macos-native job failed because xnuimagefuzzer.m imports UIKit
(87 API references), which is not available in the raw macOS SDK.

Changes:
- Use -target arm64-apple-ios17.2-macabi (Mac Catalyst clang target)
- Add -iframework for iOSSupport frameworks path
- Compile all 4 source files (was missing AppDelegate/SceneDelegate/ViewController)
- Link UIKit instead of AppKit
- Match build-native.sh flags for consistency

This keeps the Job 2 purpose (validate clang build without xcodebuild)
while using the correct target triple for UIKit-dependent code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve three wrapper generation bugs found via stress testing

1. Scan function parameter/return types for opaque struct references
   Types like sqlite3_blob only appear in function signatures (not struct
   members) and were missing forward declarations, causing UndefVarError
   at module load time.

2. Exclude enum names from forward declarations
   Enum types (e.g., http_method) defined via @enum were getting duplicate
   empty struct forward declarations that shadowed the enum definition.

3. Sanitize union accessor type names and defer emission
   - Union member type names now go through _sanitize_julia_type_name()
     to match actual struct definitions (e.g., __pthread_mutex_s → _pthread_mutex_s)
   - Unknown Ptr{X} inner types fall back to Ptr{Cvoid}
   - Union accessor functions are deferred to after all struct definitions
     to avoid forward reference errors

Validated against: SQLite (269 functions), cJSON (79 functions),
tinyexpr (7 functions), http-parser (15 functions with enums),
Duktape (JS engine), and the existing test suite (81 tests pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  usage, format, and when to regenerate the atlas
- New 'make gen-atlas' target in Makefile for building the tool
- Updated Makefile header comments to list the new target

The gen-atlas tool generates 256×256 SDF atlases for text rendering
(95 ASCII printable chars + replacement glyph). Previously undocumented
but referenced in ROADMAP Phase 1.4.

Addresses audit findings:
- gen-atlas tool undocumented in README
- No build verification for gen-atlas
(AUDIT-2026-03-07.md)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- ReviewCard: shows teacher.name + department, removed tag pill

- AdminPage: teacher count shown in tab label and overview stats
- All 17 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CFL-073: icMBBFromXml channel count validation (CWE-122)

Root cause: icMBBFromXml() passes full int nIn/nOut from XML attributes
to icCurvesFromXml(), but NewCurvesA/B/M() allocates using icUInt8Number
(truncates to 0-255). With OutputChannels="6914", allocation =
6914 & 0xFF = 2 pointers (16 bytes), but loop iterates up to 6914.

Fix: Validate nIn/nOut in range [1,16] before Init(). ICC profiles
support max 16 channels per color space; values outside this range
indicate malformed XML.

Both iccFromXml tool AND icc_fromxml_fuzzer crash identically — this
is a valid upstream bug, not a fidelity gap.

Crash: heap-buffer-overflow in icCurvesFromXml() at IccTagXml.cpp:3330
Trigger: crash-a39b6ce2b74a007bc4096bf1b49589f0a3bbc23d (4541B XML)
CWE-122: Heap-based Buffer Overflow via integer truncation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
